_comment: The Accountability Ledger. Each entry is a concrete direction the Supreme Court issued to a government duty-bearer, with the granted period converted to an absolute 'due' date. Status is computed LIVE in the dashboard from 'due' vs today, EXCEPT where an explicit 'status' is set (missed/complied/ongoing) because a later tracked order confirms it. Every 'directive' is condensed from fetched order text — never invented. 'overdue'/'due-soon'/'upcoming' are never asserted as government failure; they mean the deadline has passed with compliance unconfirmed on the tracked record.

directions.directive: No heavy or commercial vehicle to park or stop on any National Highway carriageway or paved shoulder except at designated bays; enforced through ATMS real-time alerts, GPS-timestamped evidence and e-challans.
File a consolidated report on highway inspection teams, encroachments identified/demolished, and Rajmargyatra grievance-module operationalisation with toll-free 1033.
Demolish/remove all new or existing unauthorised dhabas, eateries and structures within the Right of Way of every National Highway.
Constitute a District Highway Safety Task Force in every district the National Highway passes through, with the Collector and SP jointly responsible.

Operationalise the Advanced Traffic Management System (cameras, speed detectors, message signs, emergency call boxes) across all 4/6-lane highways and file a compliance affidavit certifying each component.
Deploy BLS ambulances and recovery cranes at intervals not exceeding 75 km on every National Highway stretch (Article 21 positive duty of emergency response).
Publish a comprehensive list of accident blackspots on National Highways and issue a policy framework mandating high-mast lighting, speed cameras and reflective markings at each.
File a consolidated district-wise and agency-wise compliance report before the Court on all of the above directions.
Conduct drone-based aerial highway surveys at least twice a year, with six-monthly reports filed before the Court.
Integrate all emergency/ambulance helplines (100, 101, 108, 102, 1033, 1091) into helpline 112, with concurrent mass-media publicity, and report compliance.
Establish functional physical and digital Good Samaritan Grievance Redressal Systems with State- and District-level nodal authorities.
Establish State Trauma Registries covering all medical facilities and link them to a Coordinated Trauma Registry (data format to be issued by MoHFW within 8 weeks).

Fully operationalise the PM RAHAT cashless-treatment scheme — designate hospitals, on-board the State Health Agency to the TMS, deploy police on eDAR — non-implementation declared a violation of the MV Act.
Constitute the National Road Safety Board under Section 215B of the Motor Vehicles Act (six months granted).
directions.status_note: 13 May 2026 order records the Board was not constituted; a final three-month extension was granted.

directions.directive: Constitute the National Road Safety Board — 'one last opportunity' after the 2025 deadline lapsed; NRSB Rules 2025 already framed.
Report compliance on clearing National Highway land of encroachments, with a toll-free complaint number and action-taken reports uploaded to a portal.
File an affidavit on the grievance-redressal machinery under the Control of National Highways (Land and Traffic) Act 2002, with wide publicity of the mechanism.
